name: DNA complementary base for adenine
question_text: In double-stranded DNA, which base pairs with adenine on the opposite strand?
general_feedback: Watson–Crick base pairing: adenine (A) pairs with thymine (T) via two hydrogen bonds; guanine (G) pairs with cytosine (C) via three hydrogen bonds. In RNA, uracil replaces thymine.
correct_feedback: Correct — A pairs with T in DNA.
incorrect_feedback: Incorrect — remember the Watson–Crick pairing rules for DNA.

answers.feedback: Right — A–T pair via two hydrogen bonds in DNA.
Uracil replaces thymine in RNA, not in DNA.
Guanine pairs with cytosine, not with adenine.
Cytosine pairs with guanine in the other Watson–Crick pair.
